puzzle.title: The Voice from the City's Edge
puzzle.intro: When messengers of truth arrived in a city, they were met with denial and threats. From the outskirts, a lone figure emerged, their heart filled with conviction. This individual raced to defend the messengers and call their own people to guidance, a courageous act that would seal their fate. Can you identify this soul and the message they championed?
clues: I lived in a city that was visited by divine messengers, but my people were skeptical and hostile towards them.
I heard of the messengers' plight and their message of worshipping the One Creator.
From the farthest part of the city, I came running to support them.
I addressed my people directly, questioning why they wouldn't worship the One who created them and to whom they would return.
I declared my own faith openly, saying, 'Indeed, I have believed in your Lord, so listen to me.'
My people responded to my plea with violence, but my reward was immediate. Upon my death, it was said to me, 'Enter Paradise.'

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: The Believer from Fir'awn's Family
Luqman the Wise
The Believer of Ya-Sin
A Disciple of 'Isa
Zayd ibn Harithah

trial.label: What Was My Primary Action?
options: Concealing my faith in a tyrant's palace
Rushing to defend messengers and advise my people
Interpreting the dreams of a ruler
Building a great wall to contain a corrupting force
Debating a king about the power of my Lord
trial.answer: Rushing to defend messengers and advise my people

location.label: Key Context
options: A city visited by three messengers
The court of the Pharaoh of Egypt
A cave where we slept for centuries
The valley of Tuwa by a sacred fire
The city of Makkah during an elephant attack
location.answer: A city visited by three messengers

outcome.label: What Was My Immediate Reward?
options: Being given wisdom and knowledge
Being raised to a high station
Being saved from a fire that became cool
Being told 'Enter Paradise'
Being reunited with my long-lost family

puzzle.arabic: قِيلَ ٱدْخُلِ ٱلْجَنَّةَ ۖ قَالَ يَـٰلَيْتَ قَوْمِى يَعْلَمُونَ
puzzle.verse: It was said, "Enter Paradise." He said, "I wish my people could know (36:26)
